page: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is configured with 6 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. This processor features 3 physical cores with 2 threads per core, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the FX24-6ms_v2 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities. It achieves a top-tier rating in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, alongside strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% of tested configurations, which is also reflected in a lower Passmark memory score. Multi-core performance, static web serving, and database operations generally align with mid-range averages.

Given its massive memory capacity relative to its 6 vCPUs, this server represents a highly specialized resource density profile. It provides high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling, such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. Conversely, it is less suited for large-scale LLM inference or highly parallelized multi-threaded computing due to its modest core count and lower large-block memory bandwidth.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server instance from the FXmsv2 family, featuring 6 dedicated vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by high Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmark scores. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but supports premium storage options. While its single-threaded processing and memory capacity are exceptionally strong, its large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling are more moderate. This hardware profile makes the FX24-6ms_v2 highly efficient for memory-intensive, low-core workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and specialized enterprise applications requiring massive memory density per core.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz and 504 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and high single-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high memory-to-core ratio server featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processors for memory-intensive and single-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 6 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
504.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptional 84.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU, but compatible with premium storage
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound applications
